claims.claim: Hebcal default candle lighting is 18 minutes before sunset; Jerusalem uses 40 minutes, Haifa and Zikhron Ya'akov use 30 minutes.
claims.raw_snippet: b=18 - Candle-lighting time minutes before sunset. By default, candle lighting time is 18 minutes before sundown ... variations for specific locations like Jerusalem (40 min) and Haifa (30 min).

claims.claim: Hebcal default Havdalah with M=on is Tzeit HaKochavim, sun 8.5 degrees below horizon. Fixed-minute alternatives: m=42 medium stars, m=50 small stars, m=72 Rabbeinu Tam.
claims.raw_snippet: tzeit hakochavim (when 3 small stars are visible, sun 8.5 below horizon) ... 42 minutes for three medium-sized stars, 50 minutes for three small stars, 72 minutes for Rabbeinu Tam, 0 to suppress.

claims.claim: Hebcal API rate-limits at 90 requests per 10-second window, returning HTTP 429.
claims.raw_snippet: Rate-limiting is used to throttle clients ... You may receive a 429 'Too Many Requests' error if your client makes more than 90 requests in a 10-second window.

claims.claim: Pesach 2026 runs April 1 to April 8 (first day April 2, last day April 8) in Israeli observance.
Shavuot 2026 begins evening Thursday May 21 and ends evening Friday May 22, one-day Yom Tov in Israel.
Rosh Hashana 2026: Friday September 11 (eve) through Sunday September 13. Combined with adjacent Shabbat this creates a three-day no-work span.
claims.raw_snippet: Rosh Hashana - Fri, Sept 11 - Sun, Sept 13 (multi-day span)

claims.claim: Yom Kippur 2026: evening Sunday September 20 through evening Monday September 21.
claims.raw_snippet: Yom Kippur - Sun, Sept 20 - Mon, Sept 21

claims.claim: Sukkot 2026 in Israel: Friday September 25 (eve) through Friday October 2. Shemini Atzeret/Simchat Torah on October 2-3 (combined in Israel).
claims.raw_snippet: Sukkot - Fri, Sept 25 - Fri, Oct 2 ... Shemini Atzeret & Simchat Torah - Fri, Oct 2 - Sat, Oct 3

claims.claim: Tisha B'Av 2026: eve Wednesday July 22 through Thursday July 23. Three Weeks period (17 Tammuz to 9 Av) runs early July to late July 2026.
claims.raw_snippet: Tisha B'Av - Wed, July 22 - Thu, July 23

claims.claim: Yom HaAtzmaut 2026 falls April 21-22 (postponed/nidcheh from the natural 5 Iyar to avoid Shabbat conflict).
claims.raw_snippet: Yom HaAtzma'ut for Hebrew Year 5786 began on Tuesday, 21 April 2026 and ended on Wednesday, 22 April 2026. ... if the 5th of Iyar is on a Monday, the festival is postponed.
Yom HaShoah - Mon, Apr 13 - Tue, Apr 14
Yom HaZikaron - Mon, Apr 20 - Tue, Apr 21

claims.claim: @hebcal/core JavaScript library is actively maintained, latest version 6.5.1 as of May 2026.
claims.raw_snippet: The latest version of @hebcal/core is 6.5.1, last published 2 days ago. ... perpetual Jewish Calendar API.

claims.claim: pyluach Python library is at version 2.3.0, low maintenance activity, but stable for Hebrew/Gregorian conversion.
claims.raw_snippet: The current version available on PyPI is 2.3.0 ... pyluach hasn't seen any new versions released to PyPI in the past 12 months, and could be considered as a discontinued project or one that receives low attention from its maintainers. However, the package receives a total of 306,840 weekly downloads and is classified as popular.

claims.claim: Legacy 'hebcal' npm package (hebcal-js) is deprecated, succeeded by @hebcal/core.
claims.raw_snippet: hebcal-js: DEPRECATED - a perpetual Jewish Calendar (JavaScript)

claims.claim: HebCal Shabbat times REST endpoint: https://www.hebcal.com/shabbat with b= (candle-lighting minutes) and M=on or m=N (havdalah) parameters.
claims.raw_snippet: Endpoint URL: https://www.hebcal.com/shabbat ... b=18 candle-lighting minutes ... M=on for Tzeit HaKochavim ... m=N for fixed minutes.

claims.claim: HebCal holiday list endpoint uses i=on flag to return Israeli (one-day Yom Tov) observance instead of diaspora two-day.
claims.raw_snippet: i=on - Israeli holiday schedule (1-day Yom Tov instead of diaspora 2-day).
